summary: In a philosophical dialogue with J. Krishnamurti, Iris Murdoch explores the nature of experience, desire, and love. While questioning Krishnamurti's rejection of psychological becoming, Murdoch articulates her own views on the divided nature of the self, the continuum of good and bad, and the role of purified desire and art in achieving selfless experience.
themes: The continuum of good and bad

principles.statement: Becoming good is a matter of purifying one's desires.
principles.rationale: Desire is a fundamental source of human energy. Rather than eliminating desire, moral progress involves cultivating good desires directed toward good ends.
principles.source_quote: I would think that becoming good... is a matter of purifying one's desires, having good desires, desiring something which is good.

principles.rationale: It is a basic characteristic of human consciousness that we experience internal conflict, where one part of ourselves judges, disapproves of, or attempts to control another part.
principles.source_quote: one doesn't have to be philosopher or hold a religious view to think that one is divided, one is a divided person. And there are times when one part of you disapproves of another part.

principles.rationale: When a person truly attends to a great work of art, their ego or self disappears, leaving only the object of attention.
principles.source_quote: I would think that people have what I would call selfless experience when – well for instance when they are looking at a great work of art.

principles.statement: Good and bad exist on a continuum in human beings.
principles.rationale: Unlike abstract concepts of perfection, in real human conditions, good and bad blend together, making it difficult to distinguish them absolutely.
principles.source_quote: On the other hand in a human being good and bad grade into each other, and sometimes you don't know which is which.

mental_models.name: The Divided Self
mental_models.description: A lens for viewing human consciousness where the self is split into parts that can judge, disapprove of, or attempt to control one another.
mental_models.example: When a person condemns themselves for being envious, demonstrating that one part of the self is judging another.

mental_models.name: The Spectrum of Good and Bad
mental_models.description: Viewing morality in human beings not as absolute, divorced opposites, but as a continuum where traits grade into one another.
mental_models.example: Recognizing that in ordinary fallen human conditions, love often occasions hate, and good and bad can be difficult to separate.

signature_quotes.text: I would think that people have what I would call selfless experience when – well for instance when they are looking at a great work of art.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing situations where the 'experiencer' is absent during an experience.

signature_quotes.text: I think desire is a source of energy. Good desire is a source of good energy...
signature_quotes.context: Challenging Krishnamurti's view that desire is inherently tied to conflict and should be understood without moral division.

signature_quotes.text: ...becoming good... is a matter of purifying one's desires, having good desires, desiring something which is good.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining her view on psychological becoming and moral improvement.

signature_quotes.text: ...in a human being good and bad grade into each other, and sometimes you don't know which is which.
signature_quotes.context: Arguing against the idea that good and bad are totally divorced from one another in human life.

signature_quotes.text: ...one doesn't have to be philosopher or hold a religious view to think that one is divided, one is a divided person.
signature_quotes.context: Asserting the reality of internal psychological conflict.

anti_patterns.description: Viewing good and bad as entirely divorced in human beings.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: In ordinary human conditions, good and bad grade into each other, and seemingly pure emotions like love often occasion hate or involve desire.
